citations.desc: Environment enhancement to promote psychological well-being.
citations.narrative:
The licensee acquired 2 emu chicks since the last inspection. Although the emu chicks were socially housed and were
provided with materials to enhance their environment, the written bird enrichment plan approved by the Attending
Veterinarian does not address special considerations for nestlings, chicks, or fledglings based on the needs of young
birds of this species.
Per this Section, exhibitors must develop, document, and follow a species-appropriate plan for environment enhancement
to promote the psychological well-being of birds. The plan must be approved by the Attending Veterinarian and at a
minimum, the plan must address each of the following: social grouping, environmental enrichment, special considerations,
restraint devices, and exemptions. The Licensee needs to develop an environmental enrichment plan that addresses the
special considerations for emu nestlings, chicks, or fledglings and the revised plan must be approved by the Attending
Veterinarian. Correct by 5/22/25.
This inspection and exit briefing were conducted with facility representative.
*END OF REPORT*
n

narrative:
3.154(c)(1) 
   
Environment enhancement to promote psychological well-being. 
The licensee acquired 2 emu chicks since the last inspection. Although the emu chicks were socially housed and were 
provided with materials to enhance their environment, the written bird enrichment plan approved by the Attending 
Veterinarian does not address special considerations for nestlings, chicks, or fledglings based on the needs of young 
birds of this species. 
 
Per this Section, exhibitors must develop, document, and follow a species-appropriate plan for environment enhancement 
to promote the psychological well-being of birds. The plan must be approved by the Attending Veterinarian and at a 
minimum, the plan must address each of the following: social grouping, environmental enrichment, special considerations, 
restraint devices, and exemptions. The Licensee needs to develop an environmental enrichment plan that addresses the 
special considerations for emu nestlings, chicks, or fledglings and the revised plan must be approved by the Attending 
Veterinarian. Correct by 5/22/25. 
 
This inspection and exit briefing were conducted with facility representative.  
 
*END OF REPORT*
